title: potentilla recta subsp. semilaciniosa
intro: Potentilla recta subsp. semilaciniosa, also known as the thorny cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family Rosaceae, characterized by its thorny stems and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Potentilla recta subsp. semilaciniosa include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
